samples.xml_description:
<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Pulse detection logic based on input signal transitions.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="data_in">One-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating the presence of pulses.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="IDLE">Initial state.</state>
        <state id="PULSE_START">State indicating the start of a pulse.</state>
        <state id="PULSE_MIDDLE">State indicating the middle of a pulse.</state>
        <state id="PULSE_END">State indicating the end of a pulse.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="state" type="internal_signal">Register to store the current state.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" type="internal_signal">Register to store the next state.</reg>
        
        <always_comb>
            case(state)
                IDLE: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b1)
                        next_state = PULSE_START;
                    else
                        next_state = IDLE;
                end
                
                PULSE_START: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b0)
                        next_state = PULSE_MIDDLE;
                    else
                        next_state = PULSE_START;
                end
                
                PULSE_MIDDLE: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b1)
                        next_state = PULSE_END;
                    else
                        next_state = IDLE;
                end
                
                PULSE_END: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b0)
                        next_state = IDLE;
                    else
                        next_state = PULSE_START;
                end
            endcase
        </always_comb>
        
        <always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)> // Sequential logic block
            if(~rst_n)
                state <= IDLE;
            else
                state <= next_state;
        </always_ff>
    </logic_description>
    
    <implementation>
        Always block triggered on the positive edge of clk or the negative edge of rst_n.
        Inside the always block, use a case statement to handle different states.
        Depending on the current state, update the next_state variable based on the conditions specified in the if statements.
        In the sequential logic block, update the state register with the next_state value on the positive edge of clk or the negative edge of rst_n.
    </implementation>
</module>
